content:
The user wants me to extract the content of the provided image and format it into a JSON structure. **1. Analyze the image:** The image is a data sheet for a Texas Instruments component, the TLV757P. It contains: - Header information (ordering, documentation, design, support). - Company logo and name. - Document identifiers (SBVS322C, date). - Title of the document. - "1 Features" section with a bulleted list. - "3 Description" section with text. - "2 Applications" section with a bulleted list. - A circuit diagram labeled "Typical Application". - A graph showing "Start-Up Waveform". - A table labeled "Package Information". - Footnotes for the table. - A footer with an important notice. **2. Extract text and structure:** * **Header:** * Ordering & quality logo * Technical documentation logo * Design & development logo * Support & training logo * Texas Instruments logo * TLV757P * SBVS322C – OCTOBER 2017 – REVISED MARCH 2024 * **Title:** * TLV757P 1A, Low $I_Q$, Small-Size, Low-Dropout Regulator * **1 Features:** * SOT-23 (DYD) package with $60.3^{\circ}\text{C/W}$ $R_{\theta JA}$ available * Input voltage range: 1.45V to 5.5V * Available in fixed-output voltages: * 0.6V to 5V (50mV steps) * Low $I_Q$: $25\mu\text{A}$ (typical) * Low dropout: * 425mV (maximum) at 1A ($3.3\text{V}_{\text{OUT}}$) * Output accuracy: 1% (maximum) * Built-in soft-start with monotonic $V_{\text{OUT}}$ rise * Foldback current limit * Active output discharge * High PSRR: 45dB at 100kHz * Stable with a $1\mu\text{F}$ ceramic output capacitor * Packages: * 2.9mm × 2.8mm SOT-23-5 (DBV) * 2.9mm × 2.8mm SOT-23-5 (DYD) with thermal pad * 2mm × 2mm WSON-6 (DRV) * **3 Description:** * The TLV757P low-dropout regulator (LDO) is an ultra-small, low quiescent current LDO that sources 1A with good line and load transient performance. The TLV757P is optimized for a wide variety of applications by supporting an input voltage range from 1.45V to 5.5V. To minimize cost and solution size, the device is offered in fixed output voltages ranging from 0.6V to 5V. This range supports the lower core voltages of modern microcontrollers (MCUs). Additionally, the TLV757P has a low $I_Q$ with enable functionality to minimize standby power. This device features an internal soft-start to lower the inrush current. This feature provides a controlled voltage to the load and minimizes the input voltage drop during start-up. When shutdown, the device actively pulls down the output to quickly discharge the outputs and provides a known start-up state. * **2 Applications:** * Set-top boxes, TV, and gaming consoles * Portable and battery-powered equipment * Desktops, notebooks, and ultrabooks * Tablets and remote controls * White goods and appliances * Grid infrastructure and protection relays * Camera modules and image sensors * **Typical Application Diagram:** * Shows a block diagram of the TLV757P with inputs (IN, EN), outputs (OUT), and connections to capacitors ($C_{IN}$, $C_{OUT}$). * **Graph:** * Title: Start-Up Waveform * X-axis: Time (ms) * Y-axis (left): Voltage (V) * Y-axis (right): Output Current (mA) * Curves: $V_{OUT}$, $V_{IN}$, $V_{EN}$, $I_{OUT}$ * **Package Information Table:** * Columns: PART NUMBER, PACKAGE$^{(1)}$, PACKAGE SIZE$^{(2)}$ * Rows: * TLV757P | DRV (WSON, 6) | 2mm × 2mm * TLV757P | DBV (SOT-23, 5) | 2.9mm × 2.8mm * TLV757P | DYD (SOT-23, 5) | 2.9mm × 1.6mm * **Footnotes:** * (1) For more information, see the Mechanical, Packaging, and Orderable Information. * (2) The package size (length × width) is a nominal value and includes pins, where applicable. * **Footer:** * IMPORTANT NOTICE regarding intellectual property and production data. **3. Format as JSON:** Construct the JSON object based on the extracted information. Ensure proper escaping of special characters and formatting. ```json [{"bbox": [232, 7, 346, 35], "category": "header", "text": "Ordering & quality"}, {"bbox": [367, 9, 496, 37], "category": "header", "text": "Technical documentation"}, {"bbox": [515, 7, 640, 35], "category": "header", "text": "Design & development"}, {"bbox": [659, 7, 766, 35], "category": "header", "text": "Support & training"}, {"bbox": [85, 55, 248, 88], "category": "header", "text": "TEXAS INSTRUMENTS"}, {"bbox": [847, 64, 913, 78], "category": "header", "text": "TLV757P"}, {"bbox": [616, 77, 913, 89], "category": "header", "text": "SBVS322C – OCTOBER 2017 – REVISED MARCH 2024"}, {"bbox": [228, 94, 771, 116], "category": "title", "text": "TLV757P 1A, Low \\(I_{Q}\\), Small-Size, Low-Dropout Regulator"}, {"bbox": [89, 135, 193, 152], "category": "title", "text": "1 Features"}, {"bbox": [89, 159, 437, 189], "category": "text", "text": "• SOT-23 (DYD) package with $60.3^{\\circ}$C/W $R_{\\theta JA}$ available"}, {"bbox": [89, 190, 367, 205], "category": "text", "text": "• Input voltage range: 1.45V to 5.5V"}, {"bbox": [89, 205, 362, 220], "category": "text", "text": "• Available in fixed-output voltages:"}, {"bbox": [111, 222, 320, 237], "category": "text", "text": "– 0.6V to 5V (50mV steps)"}, {"bbox": [89, 237, 276, 252], "category": "text", "text": "• Low $I_{Q}$: $25\\muA$ (typical)"}, {"bbox": [89, 253, 212, 267], "category": "text", "text": "• Low dropout:"}, {"bbox": [111, 269, 397, 285], "category": "text", "text": "– 425mV (maximum) at 1A ($3.3V_{OUT}$)"}, {"bbox": [89, 285, "category": "text", "text": "• High PSRR: 45dB at 100kHz"}, {"bbox": [89, 360, 428, 375], "category": "text", "text": "• Stable with a $1\\muF$ ceramic output capacitor"}, {"bbox": [89, 376, 195, "text": "2 Applications"}, {"bbox": [89, 483, 412, 499], "category": "text", "text": "• Set-top boxes, TV, and gaming consoles"}, {"bbox": [89, 499, 414, 514], "category": "text", "text": "• Portable and battery-powered equipment"}, {"bbox": [89, 515, 388, 529], "category": "text", "text": "• Desktops, notebooks, and ultrabooks"}, {"bbox": [89, 529, 321, 544], "category": "text", "text": "• ""}, {"bbox": [212, 715, 365, 731], "category": "figure_caption", "text": "Typical Application"}, {"bbox": [130, 741, 447, 906], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [212, 915, 365, 930], "category": "figure_caption", "text": "Start-Up Waveform"}, {"bbox": [511, 135, 641, 153], "category": "title", "text": "3 Description"}, {"bbox": [509, 159, 910, 419], "category": "text", "text": "The TLV757P low-dropout regulator (LDO) is an ultra-small, low quiescent current LDO that sources 1A with good line and load transient performance. The TLV757P is optimized for a wide variety of applications by supporting an input voltage range from 1.45V to 5.5V. To minimize cost and solution size, the device is offered in fixed output voltages ranging from 0.6V to 5V. This range supports the lower core voltages of modern microcontrollers (MCUs). Additionally, the TLV757P has a low $I_{Q}$ with enable functionality to minimize standby power. This device features an internal soft-start to lower the inrush current. This feature provides a controlled voltage to the load and minimizes the input voltage drop during start-up. When shutdown, the device actively pulls down the output to quickly discharge the outputs and provides a known start-up state."}, {"bbox": [509, 424, 910, 547], "category": "text", "text": "The TLV757P is stable with small ceramic output capacitors allowing for a small overall solution size. A precision band-gap and error amplifier provides a typical accuracy of 1%. All device versions have integrated thermal shutdown, current limit, and undervoltage lockout (UVLO). The TLV757P has an internal foldback current limit that helps reduce thermal dissipation during short-circuit events."}, {"bbox": [509, 553, 910, 645], "category": "text", "text": "The TLV757 is available in the popular SON and SOT23-5 packages. This device is also available in a thermally enhanced SOT23-5 package (DYD) with a thermal pad. This package provides significantly reduced thermal resistance compared to a standard SOT23-5 package."}, {"bbox": [628, 652, 793, 666], "category": "table_caption", "text": "Package Information"}, {"bbox": [512, 666, 906, 738], "category": "table", "text": "

PRODUCTION DATA."}] ```
